summary: Frontend regression pass for the 'Alabaster Authority' light-theme redesign of SaniClock. All 6 acceptance criteria PASS end-to-end: (1) /login renders the light design — body bg rgb(247,247,245), left pane shows 'SaniClock / POWERED BY SANIXPERTS' wordmark + 'Welcome back.' heading + Username/Password fields + blue 'Sign in' button (rgb(0,68,255) === #0044FF) + '256-bit encrypted session · SaniXperts' cue; right pane shows a large live clock (02:55:15 → 02:55:16 tick), 'Friday, Jul 17, 2026' date, and 3 shift segments (Day 07:00–15:00, Afternoon 15:00–23:00, Night 23:00–07:00) — the Night card has class 'shift now', visible blue-accent border, 'ON NOW' pill, and progress '3h 55m elapsed · 4h 05m left'. All required data-testids exist (login-form, login-username-input, login-password-input, login-submit-btn, live-clock). (2) Admin admin/Saniclock2026! posts to /api/login and redirects to '/', dashboard body bg rgb(247,247,245) — light theme confirmed. (3) #navAbsence is a fully active nav item with pending badge '2'; clicking opens #absenceView; #absSeg shows 'Pending 2 | Approved 0 | Rejected 0 | All 2'; #absRows lists both seeded requests (Aisha Khan 1001 Vacation Family trip, Marco Rossi 1002 Sick Flu). Approve on first row updates segbar to 'Pending 1 | Approved 1 | Rejected 0 | All 2'. (4) /me renders light theme (html bg rgb(247,247,245), sign-in card bg #ffffff, text rgb(9,10,11)); force-show portal reveals '#openAbs' (text 'Request absence or leave'); clicking opens '#absOv' sheet with class 'ov on' containing SELECT#aType default 'Vacation', INPUT#aStart type=date, INPUT#aEnd type=date, INPUT#aReason, BUTTON#aSubmit 'Submit for approval'. (5) All 3 pages are dark-on-light and legible; no unreadable elements found (a heuristic false positive on the blue 'Sign in' button — white text on blue gradient — is fine visually). (6) Only console error observed is the single expected 401 on /api/my-punches during /me unauthenticated polling — no new JS errors on /login, /, or /me.

issues: Carryover from iteration 2 (not a new regression): the 'ON NOW' markup is emitted inside ALL three shift cards; only the '.now' (active Night) card visually shows the pill because CSS hides '.sfbadge' on non-active cards. Screen readers may announce 'ON NOW' three times. Optional a11y polish: emit .sfbadge only when cur===key.

action_items: OPTIONAL a11y polish (unchanged from iter-2): in LOGIN_HTML shift-card template, only emit the .sfbadge 'ON NOW' span when the shift is the active one (cur === key), rather than emitting it on all three and hiding two via CSS.
critical_code_review_comments: scale.js is now ~3625 lines — still a single monolith. Strongly recommend splitting LOGIN_HTML, ME_HTML, MOBILE_HTML, page() dashboard HTML, and API handlers into separate modules (e.g., views/login.js, views/me.js, views/dashboard.js, api/absence.js). Maintainability risk grows with each redesign iteration.

/me still polls /api/my-punches on load without a session and surfaces the 401 as a browser console error. Add a lightweight session probe (or catch/swallow the 401) so the console is clean for genuine debugging.
LOGIN_HTML shift-card badge is CSS-hidden rather than conditionally rendered — worse for accessibility (SR still reads the text) and marginally more DOM. Prefer conditional rendering.

The 'Alabaster' override block (line 955+) layers on top of the base :root/[data-theme=light] tokens instead of replacing them. This works but produces two sources of truth for colors; consolidating into a single theme block would reduce specificity/!important churn.

ME_HTML still declares '<meta name="theme-color" content="#05070e"/>' — a dark-theme leftover. Under the new light theme it makes the mobile browser chrome dark; consider updating to '#ffffff' or the light bg to match the rest of the redesign.
test_credentials: Admin admin / Saniclock2026! at /login — verified working. No employee accounts seeded; /me portal tested by force-showing #portalView per instructions.
seed_data_creation: Did not create any seed data. Testing did mutate data/absence-requests.json by approving Aisha Khan's request (was pending, now approved). Marco Rossi remains pending. Post-test state: 1 approved (Aisha) + 1 pending (Marco).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Alabaster Authority redesign is fully working end-to-end and functionally identical to iteration 2. Post-run absence-requests.json state = 1 approved (Aisha Khan/Vacation) + 1 pending (Marco Rossi/Sick); restore to 2 pending if the next iteration needs a clean state. Two carryover polish items remain (both optional): (a) conditional-render 'ON NOW' badge on /login, (b) update ME_HTML meta theme-color from '#05070e' to a light value.
rca of the issue: n/a — no regressions detected. All acceptance criteria met.
